t_ch: 还有我的同伴——殉道的半神们，他们会跨越轮回，成为新生的泰坦。英雄们的牺牲不会白费。
t_en: And my friends — the fallen demigods, they shall transcend the cycle to become new Titans. Our heroes shall not have died in vain.

t_ch: 当然。我可以证明。
t_en: Of course. This I can guarantee.

t_ch: 那么，第二个承诺——
t_en: Good. The second one—

t_ch: 告诉我：{NICKNAME}能不为翁法罗斯的规则制约，平安返回{F#她}{M#他}的家乡。
t_en: Promise me {NICKNAME} can return safely to {M#his}{F#her} homeland, unbound by Amphoreus's laws.

t_ch: …很遗憾，我无法对此做出回应。如你方才所说，{M#他}{F#她}已接过「岁月」的权柄……
t_en: ...My apologies, but I cannot promise this. As you mentioned, {F#s}he now bears the Time Titan's authority...

t_ch: {M#他}{F#她}涉入此世，已然太深。
t_en: {M#His}{F#Her} bonds with this world run too deep.

t_ch: 不用担心，我自有办法。
t_en: No need to worry, I have a plan.

t_ch: 开启再创世吧，相信我。
t_en: Trust me, initiate the Era Nova.

t_ch: 哦…是我见识浅薄了。看来，身为天外之人，{M#他}{F#她}的确有超越命运的手段。
t_en: Well... I stand corrected. An outsider like {M#him}{F#her} does have ways to defy destiny.

t_ch: …感谢你们为这个世界所付出的一切，伙伴。即便来世无人记念，我也一定会铭记在心。
t_en: ...Thank you, my friends, for everything you've done for this world. Though the next world may not remember, I shall never forget.

t_ch: 以神礼观众之名，我见到：翁法罗斯已选择了她的命运。
t_en: As the Theoros, I declare: Amphoreus has determined her path.

t_ch: 上前来，英雄们——请将她的命运付诸实现吧。
t_en: Step forward, heroes — follow her path and see it through.

t_ch: 在此世行将终结之际——以神礼观众之名，向您致意。
t_en: As our world draws its final breath... I stand before you as the Theoros.

t_ch: 在仪式开始前，我想问…
t_en: Before we begin... there's something I need to know.

t_ch: 你究竟是什么身份？
t_en: Who are you, really?

t_ch: 请允许我重新自我介绍：我名为吕枯耳戈斯，过去是为奥赫玛元老院的名誉元老。身为「神礼观众」，我将见证并捍卫奥赫玛全体公民自由，直到最后一刻。
t_en: Allow me to reintroduce myself. I am Lycurgus, once an honored elder of the Okhema Council. As the Theoros, I have sworn to watch over and protect every citizen of Okhema until the very end.

t_ch: 当然，我知道仅是这样的回答不能满足阁下的好奇心。因此，我愿附上这样一条信息——它对翁法罗斯而言并无价值，但对您这样的天外行者则意义重大。
t_en: Of course, that alone won't satisfy your curiosity. So let me share something more... something that means little to Amphoreus, but everything to a traveler like yourself.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>吕枯耳戈斯是万千「智识」行者的一员：它代博识尊将目光投向此地，以免翁法罗斯落入衰败的命运。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>I, Lycurgus, am one of the Erudition's many pathstriders. Through me, Nous keeps watch over this realm, working to save Amphoreus from decay.</color>

t_ch: 我不求凭只言片语能换取阁下的信任，但请至少相信：从宏观角度而言，你我所行的道路并无区别。
t_en: I don't expect you to trust me based on words alone. But believe this much: When you look at the bigger picture, we're walking the same path, you and I.

t_ch: 你到底隐瞒了什么？
t_en: What aren't you telling me?

t_ch: 呵呵…就算圣城将我奉为权要，然而之于天地，我也不过渺然一身，所能及者量小力微。
t_en: Ah... While the holy city may honor me, I'm but a humble speck before the cosmos. My reach... is limited.

t_ch: 我充分理解阁下如今满腹疑问。对此，我只能向您如此作保——
t_en: Your doubts are natural. Let me assure you of this—

t_ch: 那过去的，我已如实陈述；那未来的，我将公平见证。对于神礼观众之名，还有翁法罗斯和她的命运，我问心无愧。
t_en: What's done, I've told truthfully. What's to come, I'll witness fairly. My conscience is clear... to my role as the Theoros, to Amphoreus and her destiny.

t_ch: 你和螺丝咕姆是什么关系？
t_en: What's your connection to Screwllum?

t_ch: 于我眼中，这世间一切不以血肉为基的生命皆同根同源——我们都诞生于宇宙间恒常且神圣的数学法则。
t_en: I believe, all synthetic life shares a common source — we're all children of the universe's eternal mathematical law.

t_ch: 接此逻辑，答案便已明朗：对我而言，那位天才是同胞，亦是「兄弟」。
t_en: So you see, the answer is simple: that brilliant mind is my kindred, a "brother," if you will.

t_ch: 就到这里吧，该完成仪式了。
t_en: Let's leave it at that. Time to complete the ritual.

t_ch: 世界将为您的奉献动容，开拓者。
t_en: The world will be moved by your sacrifice, Trailblazer.

t_ch: 该完成仪式了。
t_en: It's time to complete the ritual.

t_ch: 世界将为您的奉献动容，开拓者。
t_en: The world will be moved by your sacrifice, Trailblazer.

t_ch: 如果没有你，我们根本无法迎来预言中的明天……
t_en: Without you, we could never have reached our prophesied tomorrow...

t_ch: …感谢你陪我走到最后一刻。
t_en: Thank you for walking this path with me to the end.

t_ch: 众神啊，看哪！翁法罗斯已完成了她的胜利，再创世即将到来——
t_en: Behold, O gods! Amphoreus has achieved her victory, and the Era Nova approaches—

t_ch: 那辉煌的灵魂已临到此地，行走过熙熙攘攘的黑夜；他携来黄金的火与血，胜利地步入白昼——
t_en: The radiant soul has arrived, having walked through the bustling nights, bearing the golden fire and blood, stepping with pride into daylight—

t_ch: 他是纯洁的孩童，向昨日、今日和明日的大道上走去——
t_en: He is the pure child, walking upon the path of yesterday, today, and tomorrow—

t_ch: 他是未生者之初生，他是无名者之初名——
t_en: He is the first to be born, first to be named among the nameless—

t_ch: 他的躯体是永恒，他的四肢是无尽，他将绵延的黑夜踏在脚下——
t_en: Eternal in form, limitless in reach, he treads the endless night underfoot—

t_ch: 他是诸王中的至高，流离者的牧人，将团结的人子高举于仇敌之上——
t_en: Greatest of kings, protector of the displaced, raising humanity's children above their adversaries—

t_ch: 熔金的苍穹在他的脊椎和肌腱中奔涌——
t_en: The firmament of molten gold courses through his very bones and flesh—

t_ch: 最壮丽的诡计也在他的呼吸与言辞里显形——
t_en: The greatest of schemes reveal against his breath and words—

t_ch: 在那美丽的新世界，耀眼黄金的湖水中，他将洁净身体——
t_en: In the golden lakes of that beautiful new world, he shall wash himself clean—

t_ch: 然后，就在那里，完成你我最初…也是最后的心愿……
t_en: There, finish what we first... and last wished for...

t_ch: …为这个我们深爱的世界，写下不同以往的结局吧。
t_en: ...To write an ending unlike any before for this world we so deeply love.

t_ch: 来吧，{NICKNAME}，和我一起。
t_en: Come, {NICKNAME}, join me.

t_ch: 与白厄一起归还最后的火种。
t_en: Restore the final Coreflame with Phainon.

t_ch: 至此，让我们所有人为灭亡预备……
t_en: The time has come for us all to face oblivion...

t_ch: 或是踏上最后的伟大征程。
t_en: ...or embark on one final grand journey.

t_ch: 话虽如此，这命题似乎有些宏大，该从何说起才好？
t_en: My, what a broad topic this is. Where shall we start?

t_ch: 有了。不妨从宇宙的起点开始吧，一场爆炸后，基本粒子诞生，演化出万事万物……
t_en: Got it. Let's start at the very beginning. The big bang, the birth of particles, the emergence of all things...

t_ch: 呵呵。开个玩笑，我想说的是，无论时间、空间、物质…所有概念，都仰赖你我的认识而存在。这便是「智识」，若没有它，宇宙只是本混沌的书，在偶然中写成，却无读者品鉴。
t_en: Just kidding. What I mean to say is that time, space, matter... all concepts exist solely because we perceive them. This is called the "Erudition." Without it, the universe would merely be a chaotic book, written by chance, yet unread by anyone.

t_ch: 你熟悉的阿那克萨戈拉斯阁下，也提出过类似的观点：翁法罗斯的一切，都是凭借「智种」在后人的记忆中诞生。
t_en: As your acquaintance Lord Anaxagoras suggested: everything in Amphoreus exists through Nouspores, living in the memories of those yet to come.

t_ch: 多么奇妙的一致性啊，寥寥数笔就勾勒出了世界的真理。也只有他能解明「『我们』究竟为何物」这一课题。
t_en: How beautifully it aligns, revealing universal truth in such simple terms. Only he could solve the riddle of "what exactly are we"?

t_ch: 可是，还记得么？即便成为了理性的化身，他仍有一道未能解答的难题……
t_en: Oh, but you'll remember, even as the embodiment of Reason, one mystery remained beyond his grasp...

t_ch: 「那末…最初的智种，又要在谁人的记忆中生根发芽呢？」
t_en: "If that is the case, whose memories did the very first Nouspore sprout from?"

t_ch: 就像古往今来，无数贤人向众神的发问。
t_en: As Sages across ages have asked the gods.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>何为「生命的第一因」？</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>What is the "prime mover of life"?</color>

t_ch: 当然，我不准备探讨树庭的哲学，不妨把视线投向更远处吧。超脱于翁法罗斯，那被世人称作禁忌的天外……
t_en: But let's set aside the Grove's teachings for now, look further, past Amphoreus, to what they call the forbidden realms beyond the sky...

t_ch: 您，可曾听闻过「学派战争」？
t_en: Do you know of the "Scholars' Strife"?

t_ch: 在那场壮绝的，以「智识」为名义的残酷战争中，一位天才的遗产被尽数摈弃，零落在银河间。
t_en: During that grand yet brutal war waged in the name of the Erudition, a genius's legacy was abandoned, strewn across the cosmos.

t_ch: 那是名为「权杖」的，凡人难以想象的宏观演算阵列。它为模拟星神的思考而诞生，又在诞生的一瞬成了星神思考的组成。它们遍布寰宇，宛如<color=#dbc291ff>博识尊的天体神经元</color>。
t_en: The "Scepters," a macrocosmic computational array beyond mortal comprehension... Made to emulate the Aeon's thoughts, it became part of the Aeon's cognition at birth. Scattered throughout the cosmos, these arrays <color=#dbc291ff>act as Celestial-Body Neurons for Nous</color>.

t_ch: 可是很遗憾，最终「智识」抛弃了它们。此事人人皆知。
t_en: But regrettably, as everyone knows, the Erudition forsook them all.

t_ch: 但为人所不知的是：被遗忘的它们，仍在孜孜不倦地求解那神明的一问。那道穷尽战火与浩劫，无数天才——连同两位机械帝皇在内——都未能得证的难题……
t_en: But what nobody knows is that, though forgotten, they never stopped seeking the divine answer. A question that survived war and disaster, stumping even the greatest minds — including both Mechanical Emperors...

t_ch: 呵呵，这下您终于要踏入「翁法罗斯」的真相了。
t_en: Now, we come to the real truth of Amphoreus.

t_ch: 如果——我是说如果——有一台权杖完成了对<color=#dbc291ff>「生命第一因」</color>的解答……
t_en: What if — hypothetically — a Scepter found the answer to <color=#dbc291ff>"the prime mover of life"</color>...

t_ch: 如果在那空虚、冰冷而孤独的演算尽头，被「智识」星神标定的失败者，却用极其漫长的时光，亲身完成了证明……
t_en: If through endless solitary extrapolations in the cosmic cold, the one deemed as a failure by the Aeon of Erudition found proof through the ages...

t_ch: 试问：结果会如何？答案或许出乎意料地简单。
t_en: What would happen? Perhaps the answer is simpler than you'd think.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>它从垂死的神经元，升格为了真正的「生命」。而赐予它新生的，是另一尊星神的瞥视。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>From a perishing neuron, it evolved into true "life," reborn through the gaze of another Aeon.</color>

t_ch: …这是发生在久远过去、无人知晓，甚至连诸位天才都不曾听闻的轶事。
t_en: ...This is a tale from the distant past, unknown to all, even unheard by the greatest geniuses.

t_ch: 至此，请容我暂且搁置那台权杖的故事。回到最初的话题，所谓「生命的第一因」，它究竟是为何物？
t_en: Now, let's leave the Scepter's story here. Returning to where we began: What is "the prime mover of life"?

t_ch: 其实，您已经对那答案无比熟悉。
t_en: Actually, you know the answer better than you realize.

t_ch: 它就在您身旁，陪伴您走过了这段漫长的征途。
t_en: It's been by your side, accompanying you throughout this long journey.

t_ch: 此刻，它就在前方，等待着您……
t_en: Right now, it awaits you ahead...

t_ch: 在那创世的终点过后，美丽的新世界里。
t_en: In that brave new world, beyond the end of Genesis.

t_ch: 白厄…他没事吧？
t_en: Phainon... is he going to be alright?

t_ch: 请相信他拯救奥赫玛的决心。
t_en: Believe in his resolve to save Okhema.

t_ch: 看，白厄在那边！还有…咦！
t_en: Look, there's Phainon! And... wait!

t_ch: （…来古士？）
t_en: (...Lygus?)

t_ch: （<color=#dbc291ff>切记…螺丝咕姆的话。</color>）
t_en: (<color=#dbc291ff>Remember... Screwllum's warning.</color>)

t_ch: 带着这份愿望走下去吧…成为开启一切的人……
t_en: Take this wish with you... Be the one who begins it all...

t_ch: 诚如神谕所示：<color=#dbc291ff>「汝将肩负骄阳，直至灰白的黎明显著」</color>……
t_en: As the prophecy foretold: <color=#dbc291ff>"You will bear the blazing sun, until the pale dawn breaks"</color>...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>走下去…背负这个世界…直到…灰白的英雄…无名的救世主…带来黎明……</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Continue onward... bearing this world upon your shoulders... until the ashen hero... the nameless Deliverer ushers in the dawn...</color>

t_ch: 好奇怪的梦啊……
t_en: Such an odd dream...

t_ch: 总觉得…不是第一次做这样的梦了。可醒来以后，什么也回想不起来。
t_en: Strange... like I've dreamed this before. But it all slips away when I wake.

t_ch: 只记得一个声音…到底是谁在呼唤呢…？
t_en: Only a voice remains... who was that...?

t_ch: 起床啦——
t_en: Rise and shine!
...What?

t_ch: 原来是你啊，好伙伴…不好意思，一不小心睡着了。
t_en: Ah, it's you... Whoops, fell asleep there.

t_ch: 我好像做了个怪梦。想不起来，先不管了。阳光正好啊！一天才刚开始呢。
t_en: What a strange dream I had. It's gone now though. But look! Perfect sunshine! The day's still young!

t_ch: 走吧，伙伴，我们找昔涟去。昨天约好了，再陪她看一次「神谕牌」。算算时间，差不多是时候了……
t_en: Let's go find Cyrene, partner. We promised to be there for her oracle card reading today. It's right about time...

t_ch: 看你这样子，是还没睡醒啊。这样吧，你先吹吹风，清醒一下。我在边上等你。
t_en: Looking at you, someone's still half asleep. Tell you what, catch some fresh air first. I'll hang around here.

t_ch: 这是哪里…？
t_en: What is this place...?

t_ch: 再创世…完成了？
t_en: The Era Nova... is it done?

t_ch: 昔涟…？
t_en: Cyrene?

t_ch: 如何？阁下所见的风景，正是背负{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}火种的无名英雄的故乡：<color=#dbc291ff>哀丽秘榭</color>。
t_en: Beautiful, isn't it? This is <color=#dbc291ff>Aedes Elysiae</color>, home of the nameless hero who carried {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}'s Coreflame.

t_ch: 当他还是一名稚嫩的少年时，这少年心中，萌发过一连串英雄主义的幻想：他时而畅想手中的木剑是一柄沉铁……
t_en: When he was young and innocent, he had heroic dreams, imagining his wooden sword was forged of iron...

t_ch: 时而畅想木棍支着的稻草人，是传说中一根血管贯穿脊背到脚跟的巨人……
t_en: ...pretending the straw dummy propped up by a wooden pole was a giant with a single blood vessel running from spine to heel...

t_ch: 时而畅想自己会打倒巨人，成为守护世界的英雄——当然，对于年幼的他，<color=#dbc291ff>「世界」只是这小小的村庄。</color>
t_en: ...dreaming of slaying the giant and becoming a hero to save the world — though to his young mind, <color=#dbc291ff>the "world" was just this small village</color>.

t_ch: 这是白厄少年的回忆？
t_en: Are we seeing Phainon's childhood?

t_ch: 阁下很敏锐。不错，您看见的这段回忆，正是他迈向新世界的开篇……
t_en: Sharp as ever. Indeed, these memories you witness are the first steps of his journey...

t_ch: 这和再创世有什么关系？
t_en: How does this connect to Era Nova?

t_ch: 您看见的这段回忆，正是他迈向新世界的开篇。而其中，我承诺阁下的戏份一定不会少……
t_en: What you witness is the start of his path to a new world. And trust me, you play no small part...

t_ch: 看不出，他以前这么中二。
t_en: I had no idea he used to be so chuuni.

t_ch: 阁下的幽默感一如既往。我的故事并非滑稽之谈，而是他迈向新世界的开篇……
t_en: Witty as always. But this is more than just a funny story. It's the start of his path...

t_ch: 这是男人最初的记忆。为免您枯坐席间，我希望阁下将其视作一场「沉浸式戏剧」……
t_en: These are his first memories. Rather than watching, why not join in? Think of it as "immersive theater"...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>扮作贯穿白厄一生中最重要的伙伴，一位始终指引其前进、却不曾在翁法罗斯历史中留名的「无名英雄」，在最佳位置欣赏这段旅程。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Become his lifelong closest friend, witnessing his hero's journey from the best seat in the house, as the nameless hero whose unwavering guidance shaped Phainon's destiny, yet whose name was never recorded in Amphoreus's history.</color>

t_ch: 什么人能贯穿他的一生？
t_en: Who could possibly be there his entire life?

t_ch: 我具体要做些什么？
t_en: What exactly do I need to do?

t_ch: 原来你也是导演？
t_en: Oh, you direct plays now?

t_ch: 呵呵，阁下心中一定有许多疑问。不妨先跟上他的脚步吧。
t_en: Questions, questions... Let's follow along and find out, shall we?

t_ch: 在这{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}永夜之帷{RUBY_E#}包裹的小村庄，还有很多秘密在等待被发掘……
t_en: Many mysteries lurk in this village beneath the {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Veil of Evernight{RUBY_E#}...

t_ch: 随着剧目徐徐展开，相信阁下的困惑一定能迎刃而解。
t_en: Your questions will find answers as the story unfolds.

t_ch: 哈，果然在这里。
t_en: Found you!
(That's...?)

t_ch: 哎呀？这么急匆匆地过来，小秋千都被吓到啦，一晃一晃……
t_en: Goodness! Look at you rushing about... you've got the poor swing all startled...
...Hmm?

t_ch: 你是…？
t_en: Who are you...?

t_ch: 唔…有种陌生的感觉呢。
t_en: Hmm... There's something oddly unfamiliar about this.

t_ch: 是你…？
t_en: Is that you...?

t_ch: 有种似曾相识的感觉呢♪
t_en: It's that déjà vu~♪

t_ch: 是你！
t_en: It's you!

t_ch: 果然是你呀♪
t_en: I knew it was you ♪

t_ch: 怎么了…在看什么？
t_en: Something wrong? What do you see?

t_ch: 嗯～没什么，只是…忽然有种莫名的心灵感应？
t_en: Oh~ nothing much, just... suddenly felt a strange connection.

t_ch: 哎呀，你脑袋上怎么还有落叶。难道又躺在树上睡觉了，还是麦田？收拾干净再出门呀。
t_en: Look at you, leaves in your hair! Were you sleeping in a tree again, or was it the wheat fields this time? At least brush yourself off before wandering around.

t_ch: 我都长大了，只有你还把我当小孩子……
t_en: Come on, I'm not a kid anymore, you're the only one who still treats me like one...

t_ch: 是吗？虽然你的个头比我要高，但论成熟，我可远胜于你呢。
t_en: Sure, sure. You may have the height advantage, but when it comes to maturity? I'm leagues ahead of you.

t_ch: 喜欢幻想那些英雄故事，把自己当成里面的角色，可不是大人会做出的举动呀。
t_en: Still fantasizing about being in heroic adventures and imagining yourself as one of the characters. That's the kind of thing kids do, not grown-ups.

t_ch: 你说是吧？既然又在陪他胡闹…今天的人设是什么？
t_en: Don't you agree? And since you're playing along with his antics... who are you pretending to be today?

t_ch: 你好，我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Hi there, I'm {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 咳，好吧。{F#她}{M#他}今天…人设应该还没想好。
t_en: *awkward cough* Well... {F#s}he hasn't decided on a character for today yet.

t_ch: 那不如就用先前的吧：从异邦漂流过来，和你订下剑艺修习契约的勇者？
t_en: How about we stick with the usual? You know, the valiant hero from a distant shore who made a pact to learn the sword from you?

t_ch: 你好，我是星核猎手。
t_en: Greetings. I'm the Stellaron Hunter.

t_ch: 咳，好吧。{F#她}{M#他}今天是…「星核猎手」。
t_en: *clearing throat* {M#Uh...}{F#Ah...} today {F#s}he's going with "Stellaron Hunter."

t_ch: 「心和猎手」？你是准备捕获谁的心呢，嗯？
t_en: Steal-a-heart Hunter? Planning to steal someone's heart, are we?

t_ch: 怎么又换身份了？明明不久前还是从异邦漂流过来，和你订下剑艺修习契约的勇者……
t_en: Switching roles again? What happened to being the valiant hero from a distant shore who made that solemn pact to learn the sword...

t_ch: 你好，我是银河无敌球棒侠。
t_en: Greetings. I'm the Invincible Galactic Baseballer!

t_ch: 咳，好吧。{F#她}{M#他}今天是…「银河无敌球棒侠」。
t_en: *clearing throat* Uh... today {F#s}he's going with "Invincible Galactic Baseballer."

t_ch: 怎么又换身份了？明明不久前还是从异邦漂流过来，和你订下剑艺修习契约的勇者……
t_en: Switching roles again? What happened to being the valiant hero from a distant shore who made that solemn pact to learn the sword...

t_ch: …饶了我吧！旧事就别提了。
t_en: Spare me! Can we not bring this up?

t_ch: 对了，不是说要看神谕牌吗？都好久没玩占卜游戏了，怎么今天突然想起来？
t_en: Speaking of which, what about those oracle cards? We haven't done divination in forever. Why the sudden interest?

t_ch: 正要和你说呢：不久前，我在秋千上睡甜甜的觉，做了个奇怪的梦……
t_en: I was just about to tell you. Earlier, while napping on the swing, I had the strangest dream...

t_ch: 那梦里，一直有个模糊的声音，似乎在呼唤什么。
t_en: There was this mysterious voice in my dream, calling out for something...

t_ch: …你也做了这样的梦？
t_en: ...Wait, you had that dream too?

t_ch: 嗯…你也是吗？难怪醒来以后，总觉得心里惴惴不安呢。
t_en: Yes... you too? That would explain this lingering sense of unease...

t_ch: 于是，我想起了小时候常玩的「神谕牌」——虽然{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}神谕总是解读不准就是啦。
t_en: Which made me think of those old oracle cards I used to play with as a kid — though the {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#} prophecies were always hit or miss.

t_ch: 孩子们要是抽到「君王」或「勇士」之类的好牌就欢呼雀跃，抽到「魔人」或者「酒鬼」就嚷嚷着这次不算，再来一次……
t_en: Yeah, all the kids would get so excited drawing cards like "Ruler" or "Warrior," but throw fits over "Devil" or "Drunkard" and demand a redo.

t_ch: 想反悔就反悔…能占卜出真东西来吗？
t_en: If you just redo it when you don't like the result... what's the point of divination?

t_ch: 孩子总是幸福的，觉得神明注意不到哀丽秘榭的小角落，会对我们睁一只眼、闭一只眼……
t_en: Ah, the blessing of childhood. Believing the gods might not notice our little corner of Aedes Elysiae, willing to let our small deceits slide...

t_ch: 但…命运从来都是确凿不移。毕竟我们也不知道神谕里的未来，究竟是多远以后。
t_en: Still... fate is absolute. After all, we never really know just how far into the future the prophecy extends.

t_ch: 总之，先去以前大家的「秘密基地」，把神谕牌找出来吧——
t_en: Anyway, let's go to our old "secret base" and look for those oracle cards—

t_ch: 呃…当年放在哪了来着？
t_en: Um... now where did we hide those...

t_ch: 我倒是还能想起几个地点…来，我们一起去看看吧？
t_en: I can think of a few spots... Let's go check it out.

t_ch: 阳光下的谷仓，散发着麦子烂熟的气味。从麦草堆里翻出了松鼠们囤积的各类果实，还有…一个缺胳膊少腿的木头小人。
t_en: In the sunlit barn, amid the scent of ripened wheat, they found various fruits hoarded by squirrels, and... a wooden figurine missing arms and legs.

t_ch: 我雕刻到一半的小兵人？居然真的还在……
t_en: My unfinished little soldier? After all these years...

t_ch: 等等，这不是当年被皮西厄斯老师没收的那个么？嘿，这下总算又团圆了……
t_en: Hey, this is the one Mrs. Pythias confiscated! Finally back in my hands...

t_ch: 白厄，昔涟？你们往哪里去呢？
t_en: Phainon, Cyrene? Where are you two headed?

t_ch: 说什么来什么…皮西厄斯老师？学校放学了吗？
t_en: Speak of the devil... Mrs. Pythias? Is school out already?

t_ch: 呵呵，今天没开课呢。清晨用落叶向{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}问了一卜，说是孩子们今天容易受伤，就干脆给大家放假了。
t_en: No classes today. This morning, I used fallen leaves for a reading, seeking {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s wisdom. They warned of potential injuries, so I gave everyone a holiday.

t_ch: 正好，最近正是农忙的时候，就算不上课还有别的事要做。
t_en: Besides, it's harvest season. There's plenty of other work to be done even without classes.

t_ch: 哀丽秘榭的小校舍只有您一位老师，是太辛苦了。
t_en: With you being our only teacher in Aedes Elysiae, you must be working so hard.

t_ch: 都是为了学生，累点也没关系。
t_en: Anything for my students. I don't mind the work.

t_ch: 这次你们出远门，可要替老师去传说中的神悟树庭，见识见识真正的智慧园地啊……
t_en: When you travel far from here, you must visit the Grove of Epiphany for me, to see what a true haven of wisdom looks like...

t_ch: …话说回来，这谷仓里是有什么宝贝不成？看你们翻了半天了。
t_en: ...By the way, what are you looking for in here? You've been searching quite thoroughly.

t_ch: （偷偷藏起木头人。）
t_en: (Quietly hide the wooden soldier)

t_ch: 谢谢你， 好伙伴！小兵人，咱们回家！
t_en: Thank you, my friend! Time to take our soldier home!

t_ch: （沉默不语。）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: 这是…玩具？真是，多大个人了还玩玩具。
t_en: Is this... a toy? Really now, aren't we past the age for these?

t_ch: 不记得了？那好，我可要带小兵人回家啦。
t_en: She doesn't remember, eh? This little soldier's coming with me.

t_ch: （向老师举报。）
t_en: (Snitch on Phainon)

t_ch: 报告老师，是白厄的玩具！
t_en: Mrs. Pythias, it's Phainon's toy!

t_ch: 喂，伙伴，小点声啊…！
t_en: Hey, partner, keep it down...!

t_ch: 这是…玩具？真是，多大个人了还玩玩具。
t_en: Is this... a toy? Really now, aren't we past the age for these?

t_ch: 不记得了？那好，我可要带小兵人回家啦。
t_en: She doesn't remember, eh? This little soldier's coming with me.

t_ch: 好了好了，我先走了。晚上说不好要下雨，你们也早点回家吧！
t_en: Alright then, I should be going. It might rain tonight, so don't stay out too late!

t_ch: 课堂上雕的木头人，原来是藏到了这里，这可真是连我也不知道的秘密呀。
t_en: So this is where the wooden figurine you carved during class ended up. And here I thought I knew all your hiding spots.

t_ch: 别说你，我都忘得一干二净了……
t_en: To be honest, I'd forgotten all about it myself...

t_ch: 是我的，是我钓上来的！
t_en: It's mine. I caught it fair and square!

t_ch: 明明是我的！
t_en: You did not! It's mine!

t_ch: 白厄哥哥，昔涟姐姐，你们来看！我钓上来一个好大好沉的玻璃瓶！
t_en: Phainon, Cyrene, come quick! Look at this huge bottle I just caught!

t_ch: 是我的！鱼竿是我的，只是借给你用一下，所以钓上来的东西也算我的。
t_en: Give it here! My rod, my catch. I only let you borrow it, remember?

t_ch: 哦，什么好东西争来争去的？来让我看看……
t_en: What's got you two so worked up? Here, show me...

t_ch: 呃，是我小时候写的许愿瓶…怎么会这么巧？
t_en: Wait a second... this is... This is my old Wish-In-A-Bottle! How did it end up here?

t_ch: 哇！白厄哥哥小时候的心愿，我要看！
t_en: Ooh, I wanna see! What did little Phainon wish for?

t_ch: 散了散了，都不许看。
t_en: Not happening. This stays sealed.

t_ch: 披索！莉维娅！开饭了！
t_en: Piso! Livia! Dinner's ready!

t_ch: 啊，是妈妈！
t_en: Coming, Mom!

t_ch: 饿了…回去吃饭！再见啦，大哥哥、大姐姐们！
t_en: Finally. I'm starving! See you later!

t_ch: 还以为扔到海水里的许愿瓶，能把我们的心愿带到哀丽秘榭外面的世界，或者能带来一些回音……
t_en: You know what's funny? I really thought tossing that bottle into the sea would carry our wishes beyond Aedes Elysiae... maybe even bring something back to us...

t_ch: 没想到…还是被海浪推了回来啊。
t_en: Never thought... the waves would end up pushing it back ashore.

t_ch: 童年的回旋镖还是飞回来了。
t_en: Funny how the past finds its way back.

t_ch: …有点遗憾哪。
t_en: Kind of disappointing, isn't it...

t_ch: 我也想看看。
t_en: I want to read it.

t_ch: 别…就是些小孩子的胡言乱语，没什么好看的。
t_en: Don't bother... It's just some silly childish nonsense... Nothing worth reading.

t_ch: 我看这不像海，像湖。
t_en: Seems more like a lake to me than an ocean.

t_ch: 啊？那还真是…挺可惜的。
t_en: Ah... well, that would explain it. What a shame.

t_ch: 还没找过的地方…是欧洛尼斯的祝祭庭院？
t_en: The only place we haven't searched... is Oronyx's {RUBY_B#Sacrament Courtyard}ritual courtyard{RUBY_E#}?

t_ch: 绕了半天路，结果「秘密基地」就近在眼前么……
t_en: Some secret base. It was right here in front of us the whole time...

t_ch: 不好意思啦！就当是陪我出门散了个步，锻炼下身体吧？毕竟我不像你，每天运动量没那么充足。
t_en: Sorry! But look on the bright side, it was a nice walk! Unlike you, I don't have such a physically active routine.

t_ch: 小时候我们约定集合的地方无非两处：村子中间那座神像，还有这里。
t_en: Those were the days. We'd always meet either by that statue in the village or right here.

t_ch: 是呀！我还记得…你们喊我出去玩总是不走正门，爱翻墙，真是调皮！
t_en: Right! And I remember you never used the front door when you wanted me to come play. You always climbed over the wall. You were such mischief-makers!

t_ch: 像某个人一样，捉不准出现的时机，是吧？
t_en: Just like a certain someone who has a habit of showing up unexpectedly, right?

t_ch: 你在对谁说话？
t_en: Who's that supposed to be?

t_ch: 也许就是你哦♪
t_en: Maybe it's you~♪

t_ch: 我就是这么捉摸不透。
t_en: Can't pin me down, can you?

t_ch: 嘻！我猜猜，{F#她}{M#他}给出的是个调皮的答案。
t_en: Ha! I bet {F#s}he said something clever just now.

t_ch: （保持沉默…）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: 喔，已读不回啊，让人有点伤心呢。
t_en: Oh, giving me the silent treatment? How cruel!

t_ch: 咳，总之，这也是从故事书里学来的，英雄不走寻常路嘛。
t_en: *ahem* Hey, I was just copying those storybook heroes. They never take the beaten path!

t_ch: 不过，这里最近只有我一个人住，还好久没认真打理了，真让人害羞。这么多角落，该从哪里找起呢？
t_en: Still, it's embarrassing, I've been the only one here for a while, and I haven't kept up with the cleaning. With so many spots to check, where should we even start?

t_ch: 屋顶。
t_en: Let's try the roof.

t_ch: 好久没去房顶了，也许那里能有所发现？上去找一找吧。
t_en: Come to think of it, I haven't checked the roof in forever. Worth a shot, right?

t_ch: 院子里。
t_en: How about the courtyard?

t_ch: 院子里到处都是落叶……
t_en: Leaves have taken over the courtyard...

t_ch: 知道，衣服被弄脏就不好了，让我来吧。
t_en: I know, I know. We wouldn't want your clothes getting dirty. Leave the dirty work to me.

t_ch: 倒不是这个理由啦。
t_en: That's not what I meant at all.

t_ch: 地窖。
t_en: What about the cellar?

t_ch: 有这种地方吗？记忆里好像是有的，掉进去很深很深……
t_en: A cellar? I think I remember one... It was deep... so very deep...

t_ch: 如果是梦游仙境之类的幻想，和我的英雄故事也差不了多少啊。
t_en: Well, if we're talking wonderland stuff, it's not that different from my hero stories.

t_ch: 那我们分开行动吧，我在院子里找一找，阁楼上就交给你了。
t_en: Alright, let's split up. I'll handle the yard, you check the upper floor.

t_ch: 好伙伴，这里留给我，咱们比比谁先找到神谕牌。
t_en: Leave this to me, partner. Want to bet who finds the oracle cards first?

t_ch: 眼前的少女，带着捉摸不透的秘密，对你的态度非常可亲……
t_en: The girl before you carries an air of mystery, her secrets elusive, yet her demeanor toward you is exceptionally warm...

t_ch: 不禁引人思索：那份心灵感应莫非真的存在？
t_en: You can't help but wonder: Could there really be some kind of mental connection?

t_ch: 嗯…远远地望过去，还是熟悉的风景呀。
t_en: Mmm... the view here hasn't changed a bit.

t_ch: 要说哀丽秘榭最让人留恋的地方，除了大树下的秋千架，就是这儿了。
t_en: You know, besides the swing under the big tree, this has to be my favorite spot in all of Aedes Elysiae.

t_ch: 站在高处确实心情愉快。
t_en: It does lift your spirits, being up high.

t_ch: 让人忍不住想哼起儿时的歌谣呢……
t_en: You can't help but want to hum one of those old songs we used to sing...

t_ch: 「枝和叶为什么生长，风要向什么方向吹拂…♪」
t_en: "Why do leaves and branches grow, which way does the wind blow...♪"

t_ch: 远处的海，一望无际啊。
t_en: The sea stretches out as far as the eye can see...

t_ch: 被连绵的山和海包围的哀丽秘榭，小时候还在纸上画过这里的风景。
t_en: I remember drawing Aedes Elysiae as a child, nestled between the sea and the mountains.

t_ch: 每次画到海的尽头就搁下笔了，完全想象不出那之外的世界是什么模样呢。
t_en: I'd always stop where the sky met the sea. The world beyond was just too vast to imagine.

t_ch: 三、二、一…跳！
t_en: Three, two, one... jump!

t_ch: 呀！什么动静？小心一点啦……
t_en: Hey, what are you doing? Please be careful!

t_ch: 虽然我是村子里的小占卜师，不过暂时还没掌握让时间倒流的能力哦。
t_en: I'm just a small-time diviner. I can't turn back time if something happens!

t_ch: 因为自幼就能听见朦胧的神谕，村子里的人都说我是未来的{RUBY_B#欧洛尼斯}岁月{RUBY_E#}祭司。
t_en: They always said I'd become the next Priest of {RUBY_B#Oronyx}Time{RUBY_E#}, just because I heard prophecies as a child.

t_ch: 不过，占卜的次数多了，反倒会对命运产生迷茫。说着对神谕笃信不疑，但真要离开哀丽秘榭的话……
t_en: But here's the thing. The more divinations I do, the more doubt creeps in. I want to believe the prophecies, but actually leaving Aedes Elysiae...

t_ch: 不知停泊的那艘船，会把我们带到什么地方呢？
t_en: Where will that waiting ship take us, I wonder...

t_ch: 神谕牌找到了，在这里——
t_en: Here they are! The oracle cards—

t_ch: 居然动作比我们快。
t_en: Looks like he won this race.

t_ch: 怀念过去，是不是总会延误启程呢？
t_en: Guess reminiscing makes us take our time, doesn't it?

t_ch: 风景还没看够呢。
t_en: I was still taking in the scenery.

t_ch: 已经装在眼睛里啦，去到哪里都会珍藏，包括…童年的伙伴哦！
t_en: I've captured it all in my heart. This view, and most precious of all... my childhood friends!

t_ch: 来啦！果然冥冥之中，神明在聆听内心的声音啊。
t_en: There we go! Looks like the gods do listen to our thoughts.

t_ch: 找到了！看，这就是装神谕牌的盒子。
t_en: Here it is! This is the box where we kept the cards.

t_ch: 可是，牌不见了…是被谁拿走了吗？
t_en: But the cards are gone... Who could have taken them?

t_ch: …哎？盒子底下盖着的，这是什么？
t_en: Huh? What's this underneath?

t_ch: 这是什么动物的爪印？
t_en: Are these... animal footprints?

t_ch: 好别致的神谕牌。
t_en: Nice cards, all looking good.

t_ch: 不不不，这很明显是被人掉包了……
t_en: No way, someone's switched them out...

t_ch: 一二三四五，丹恒打虚卒…
t_en: One, two, three, four, five... Dan Heng and Voidranger jive...

t_ch: …我知道原文，别擅自改词！
t_en: Hey, don't go changing the words!

t_ch: 我知道啦，那群神神秘秘的小家伙…没想到是它们在调皮捣蛋。
t_en: Ah, I see now! Those sneaky little ones... So that's what they've been up to.

t_ch: 你是说，「迷路迷境」里的…？
t_en: The ones from Membrance Maze, you mean?

t_ch: 嗯，跟我来，到庭院后面去吧。
t_en: Yep. Let's head to the back of the courtyard.

t_ch: 入口是在哪儿来着……
t_en: The entrance should be around here somewhere...

t_ch: 就在这边～太久没去拜访小妖精们，连去迷境的路上都长满杂草，看不清了呢。
t_en: Right here~ The path to the Maze is hidden under all these weeds. It's been too long since we visited the fairies.

t_ch: 迷路迷境？
t_en: The Membrance Maze?

t_ch: 小妖精？
t_en: Fairies?

t_ch: 入口？
t_en: An entrance?

t_ch: 不记得了吗，伙伴？小时候我们常常去找他们玩呢。
t_en: Did you forget? We used to visit them all the time when we were little.

t_ch: 来吧，说干就干！先把眼前的杂草清理掉。
t_en: Alright, let's do this! These weeds need to go.

t_ch: 啊！对，入口就在那边，那个树洞——
t_en: There it is! The entrance — that hollow in the tree—

t_ch: 只要沿着树洞爬一小段距离，穿过最狭窄的地方，就到它们的栖息地「迷路迷境」啦。
t_en: Once you get through the tight squeeze in the hollow, you'll reach their dwelling, the Membrance Maze.

t_ch: 哈，真不知道我现在还能不能钻过去……
t_en: Haha, I wonder if I can still squeeze through there...

t_ch: 不如担心一下，自己会不会被小妖精们拒绝吧。毕竟，迷路迷境实行的是「邀请制」，只有天真无邪的孩子才能进入呢。
t_en: Maybe worry more about whether the fairies will let you in! After all the Membrance Maze has an "invitation-only" rule, only children with pure hearts are welcomed.

t_ch: 听上去像什么神秘俱乐部。
t_en: Sounds like some secret society, huh?

t_ch: 能被迷境的妖精认可，算是我们童年的最高荣誉了。
t_en: Getting approved by the Maze's fairies was like our childhood badge of honor.

t_ch: 爬就爬，我第一个上。
t_en: Alright then — I'll go first.

t_ch: 那就由你来开路了，伙伴。
t_en: Lead the way then, partner.

t_ch: 我有漫游签证，不怕拒签。
t_en: My Trailblazer Profile should get me in, right?

t_ch: 「漫游签证」…是什么？
t_en: "Trailblazer Profile"... What's that?

t_ch: 不管怎么说，先挑战一下树洞？
t_en: Regardless, how about we try getting through the hollow first?

t_ch: 那…你先请？
t_en: After you, then?

t_ch: 啊？好、好吧，那就我先来……
t_en: What? Oh, um... sure, I'll go first...

t_ch: 真是冒冒失失啊……
t_en: So hasty, as always...

t_ch: 不过这下…就只剩下我们咯？继续刚才的话题吗？
t_en: Looks like it's just us now... Care to finish that chat?

t_ch: 其实，从看见小妖精的可爱字条开始，我心中的预感就越发强烈……
t_en: To be honest, from the moment I saw the cute note left by the fairies, my intuition became even more certain...

t_ch: 也许，当我们再度踏入暌违多年的迷境，命运的齿轮就要开始转动了呢。
t_en: Perhaps when we step into the Maze again after all these years, the wheels of fate will finally start turning.

t_ch: 怎么突然这么说？
t_en: What makes you say that?

t_ch: 嗯…就当是少女的直觉吧。
t_en: Oh... just a girl's intuition.

t_ch: 你不会也是幕后黑手吧。
t_en: Don't tell me you're behind all this.

t_ch: …如果我是，你会怎么做呢？
t_en: What if I am... what would you do?

t_ch: 开个玩笑，没有别的意思哦。
t_en: Just kidding, don't read too much into it!

t_ch: 齿轮一旦转动，就无法回头了…
t_en: Once the wheels start turning, there's no going back...

t_ch: 嗯…只是少女的直觉，未必会成真哦。
t_en: Oh... it's just a girl's intuition. It might not mean anything.

t_ch: 但，即便命运确凿不移，无论它要将世界带向何方，只要我们能够共同面对，那就没什么好害怕的，对吧？
t_en: But you know what? Even if fate is set on its path, no matter where it steers the world... If we're together, there's nothing to worry about, right?

t_ch: 所以，如果你愿意……
t_en: So, if you're willing...

t_ch: 嗯～算啦，没什么。如果命运果真开始流转…到时再请你听听我的请求吧♪
t_en: Ah~ forget it. If the wheels do begin to turn... that's when I'll make my request♪

t_ch: 哇…真是好久不见了迷！
t_en: Woah... look who's finally back memi!

t_ch: 小白，你怎么变得好高、好大、好帅气？远远看着，完全没认出来呢。
t_en: Snowy, you've grown so tall and dashing! I couldn't even tell it was you at first!

t_ch: 毕竟我们都长大了。
t_en: We had to grow up sometime.

t_ch: 唔…小涟倒是没怎么变呢，好神奇！
t_en: But look at Reney. She hasn't changed one bit! How fascinating!

t_ch: 说不定我和徕啦迷一样，都是迷境的小妖精呢？
t_en: Perhaps I'm like you, Relimem, just another fairy of the Maze?

t_ch: 紫色的…迷迷？你染发了？
t_en: Mem's... purple now? Did you dye your fur?

t_ch: 你是不是记错了？徕啦迷的毛发一直都是薰衣草色。
t_en: Dye? Relimem's always been this lovely shade of lavender.

t_ch: 原来迷迷是个组织？
t_en: So the Mems are like a organization?

t_ch: 组织？它们的纪律应该没那么森严…在我们来之前，大家连个像样的名字都没有呢。
t_en: An organization? Hardly. They're not that organized... They didn't even have proper names before we came along.

t_ch: 名字真是一个赛一个奇怪…
t_en: The names just keep getting weirder...

t_ch: 哈哈，可不是嘛。记得那会儿初来乍到，我光记名字就记了好几天。
t_en: Tell me about it! It took me forever to get all their names straight when I first came.

t_ch: 哆啦迷、徕啦迷、咪啦迷、发啦迷…呵，真是太久没见到这些可爱的小家伙了。
t_en: Dolimem, Relimem, Milimem, Falimem... Ah, I've missed these cute little troublemakers.

t_ch: 那时我们一起围着篝火边跳边唱，畅想未来的旅途如何精彩…时过境迁，要不是再次回到这里，那些画面都快要彻底被抛在脑后了啊。
t_en: We used to gather around the fire, singing and dancing, imagining all our future journeys... Without coming back here, those memories might've faded away entirely.

t_ch: 小白真敢说呢！当时你口口声声「一定不会忘记」，结果这么久没来，还是要把大家都忘掉了。
t_en: How could you, Snowy! You swore you wouldn't forget about us, but you were gone so long you almost did!

t_ch: 好啦，好啦。他只是在学故事里的吟游诗人多愁善感，才不会真的忘记大家呢。
t_en: Oh, he's just copying those bards from his stories, being all melodramatic. He couldn't forget about you all even if he tried.

t_ch: 我们这次来，是为了找回「神谕牌」。你知道它去哪里了吗？
t_en: Actually, we're here for the oracle cards. Have you seen them?

t_ch: 神谕牌！当然知道了，就是村长大人要我们把它带回来的。
t_en: Oh, the oracle cards! Right, the chief asked us to get those!

t_ch: 村长…多哆啦迷？
t_en: Chief... you mean Dolimem?

t_ch: 它要神谕牌做什么？如果想占卜，直接告诉我就好了呀。
t_en: What do they need the cards for? If they want a reading, they could just ask me.

t_ch: 这个就不清楚了…但村长大人似乎很想亲自见你们一面来着。
t_en: I'm not sure... but Chief specifically wanted to meet with you all.

t_ch: 我想想，它现在应该在……
t_en: Let's see, they should be...

t_ch: 「枝和叶为什么生长，风要向什么方向吹拂……」
t_en: "Why do leaves and branches grow, which way does the wind blow..."

t_ch: 「迷路迷境的最深处，揭晓未来答案的地方。」
t_en: "In Membrance Maze depths we seek to find, the answers future left behind."

t_ch: 呵呵…孩子们，真是久违了。
t_en: Hehe... welcome back, my dear children.

t_ch: 还以为你是粉色的迷迷。
t_en: I thought you'd be a pink Mem.

t_ch: 粉色…在我印象里，迷路迷境似乎从来没有过粉色的妖精，不知道多哆啦迷有没有见过？
t_en: Pink ones? That's strange. I don't recall any pink fairies in the Membrance Maze. Have you seen any, Chief Dolimem?

t_ch: 神谕牌在你这儿吗？
t_en: So, are the oracle cards with you?

t_ch: 真是开门见山啊，伙伴。先听听多哆啦迷怎么说吧。
t_en: How about we hear what the chief has to say first, my impatient friend?

t_ch: 好诗，好诗！
t_en: Now that's poetry!

t_ch: 我也这么觉得，但多哆啦迷总说自己唱的不是诗，只是歌谣。
t_en: Right? Though Dolimem always says they're just songs, not poetry.

t_ch: 村长！您还是这么有精神，真是太好啦。
t_en: Oh Chief! You haven't changed a bit, still so full of life!

t_ch: 呵呵，放心吧。还记得吗？我说过，只要世上还有人能吟唱歌谣，迷境的孩子就会一直存在——
t_en: No need to fuss, my child. You haven't forgotten, have you? As long as our songs are sung, the children of the maze will live on—

t_ch: ——正如你我命运常在。
t_en: —just as our paths stay forever linked.

t_ch: 将它取走吧，孩子。我很抱歉，不得不用这种办法将你们召唤至此。
t_en: Here, take it. I'm sorry I had to resort to such methods to bring you here.

t_ch: 这是…
t_en: This is...

t_ch: …「如我所书」？
t_en: ..."As I've Written"?

t_ch: 啊，我的神谕牌册……
t_en: My book of oracle cards...

t_ch: 有您替我保管，我就放心啦。要是想让我占卜，随时乐意效劳。
t_en: Thank you for keeping it safe. You know you can always just ask if you want me to divine something.

t_ch: 不过，时隔多年未见，又以这样特别的方式指引我们前来…是出什么事了吗，村长大人？
t_en: Still... bringing us here like this after so long... Is something wrong, Chief?

t_ch: …没错。
t_en: ...Yes.

t_ch: 其实，我们最近又听到了{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}永夜之帷{RUBY_E#}的呢喃。它说，迷境很快就会迎来新的妖精……
t_en: The {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Veil of Evernight{RUBY_E#} whispers to us once more. It tells of a new fairy who will soon enter the Maze...

t_ch: 什么……
t_en: Hmm...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>没错。迷境每迎来一位新的妖精，都代表帷幕之外，那远离哀丽秘榭的广阔天地，将要经历一场天翻地覆的劫难哪。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Indeed. Every time a new fairy arrives in the maze, it means that Beyond the Veil, in the vast lands far from Aedes Elysiae, a great calamity is on the horizon.</color>

t_ch: 多哆啦迷向众人描述了更多细节……
t_en: Dolimem continues to explain in greater detail...

t_ch: 可如果是这样，我们的远行……
t_en: But if that's the case, our journey...

t_ch: …就不得不取消了。如果灾难将至，我们还是留在哀丽秘榭为好，以防万一。
t_en: ...will have to be cancelled. If disaster's coming, we should stay in Aedes Elysiae, just to be safe.

t_ch: 你们原来的安排是？
t_en: What were you planning to do?

t_ch: 那外面的世界怎么办？
t_en: What about all the people out there?

t_ch: 你问住我了…但我和昔涟两个人，什么也做不了啊。
t_en: That's... that's a good point. But what difference could Cyrene and I make?

t_ch: 我和白厄本来只打算离开村子一小段时间，学些本领回来。他要去悬锋城锻炼，我打算去雅努萨波利斯，接受命运泰坦的洗礼……
t_en: We weren't planning to be gone for long... just enough time to hone our abilities. Phainon would train in Castrum Kremnos, while I would seek the Fate Titans' blessing in Janusopolis...

t_ch: 学成之后，我们就回哀丽秘榭，保护村子的和平不受侵扰。
t_en: Then we'd return to Aedes Elysiae, to help keep our village safe.

t_ch: 没关系，留在这里也不耽误学习。剑技这种东西，靠我自己也可以领悟。
t_en: It's okay. I can still practice my swordplay here. Some things you can learn on your own.

t_ch: 呃…大概吧。
t_en: Well... probably.

t_ch: 呵，孩子，你这副表情……
t_en: That's... not very convincing...

t_ch: 我有个提议：不妨让昔涟当场为我们开牌，卜算一下吧。如果命运愿意展现底色，我们都能更安心些，不是么？
t_en: Here's a thought: Let's have Cyrene do a reading. If fate wishes to show us its path, we might find some peace of mind.

t_ch: 可是，如果占卜的结果不尽如人意，那该怎么办？
t_en: But... What if we don't like what the cards tell us?

t_ch: 那就向它抗争，直到最后。
t_en: Then we stand against it, until the very end.

t_ch: 呵…我还真从来没有想过有这种思考方式。
t_en: Heh... never thought of doing it that way before.

t_ch: 谢谢你，伙伴，就听你的——无论结果如何，就让我们共同面对吧。
t_en: Thanks, partner. We'll do it your way — whatever the cards show, we'll face it together.

t_ch: 至少能知道逃避的方向。
t_en: At least we'll know which way to run.

t_ch: 听起来还真…消极。不过，我明白你想表达的意思。
t_en: Not the most optimistic take, but I see your point.

t_ch: 谢谢你，伙伴，就听你的——无论结果如何，就让我们共同面对吧。
t_en: Thanks, partner. We'll do it your way — whatever the cards show, we'll face it together.

t_ch: 我也担心，还是别看了。
t_en: I feel the same way. Perhaps it's better not to know.

t_ch: 连你都这么说，果然还是不必开牌了吧……
t_en: Hearing that from you... maybe we really shouldn't unveil the cards...

t_ch: 还是看看吧？就算结果不那么美好，至少我们也知道了该如何努力，尽可能让它完满一些，对不对？
t_en: Why not take a look anyway? Even if it's not what we hope for, we'll know what we need to work on, don't you think?

t_ch: …好吧，你说得对。伙伴，来——无论结果如何，就让我们共同面对吧。
t_en: You're right. Come on, partner — whatever the cards show, we'll face it together.

t_ch: 虔诚的叩问会得到同等重量的回应。
t_en: Ask with a true heart, and truth shall answer in kind.

t_ch: 来吧，闭上眼，轻轻地打开神谕牌册……
t_en: Close your eyes, and let's open the Oracle Card Book together...

t_ch: 让你的思绪像河水上漂着的星星一样流淌，趁着萤火和微风，你们的命运也将流向同样的未来……
t_en: Let your thoughts flow like stars drifting on the river, carried by fireflies and gentle winds, your destinies flowing towards the same future...

t_ch: 现在，来抽牌吧？
t_en: Now, shall we begin?

t_ch: 呀，没想到能得到如此多的回应……
t_en: Oh... Oh my... the cards are quite responsive today...

t_ch: 那就任意翻开神谕牌，看看未来将如何示现吧。
t_en: Very well, let's see what future the oracle cards wish to show us.

t_ch: 翻看第一页上的神谕牌。
t_en: Turn to the first page of oracle cards.

t_ch: 翻开「织者」。
t_en: Reveal the "Weaver."

t_ch: 「织者」牌哪，是牵连起「黄金之茧」的浪漫丝线，将众人的命运编织成一匹金灿灿的流丝，连通向一个最为光辉、烂漫的未来的牌面……
t_en: Behold, the "Weaver." It connects the romantic threads of the "Chrysalis of Gold," weaving all destinies into golden silk that flows toward the most brilliant, magnificent future...

t_ch: 以织与剑的轮舞开篇，那便以同样的优雅谢幕吧。
t_en: A dance of thread and blade set the stage, so shall it end as gracefully as it started.

t_ch: 好华丽啊…金色的织者，听名字肯定是豪门贵族出身。
t_en: Such elegance... The Gold Weaver must be of noble birth.

t_ch: 这般高贵的身份，大概与我这种人无缘…吧。
t_en: Such high standing... I doubt someone like me could ever reach it.

t_ch: 翻开「门匠」。
t_en: Reveal the "Gatekeeper."

t_ch: 这是「门匠」，她能在万千门径之中，为人们指点出正确的道路。
t_en: This is the "Gatekeeper," who guides people to their true path among myriad passages.

t_ch: *我们*抹去自己的千重耳目，只为更专注聆听圣殿里的呢喃！
t_en: We have surrendered our countless senses, dedicating ourselves to the sacred whispers within the temple!

t_ch: 好像真的听到了神殿里的回音，还混杂着…童声的歌谣？
t_en: Wait... It almost feels like I can hear the echoes within a temple! And... are those children singing?

t_ch: 让人感觉肃穆非凡，却又心生亲近……
t_en: It feels overwhelmingly solemn, yet strangely comforting...

t_ch: 翻开「君王」。
t_en: Reveal the "Ruler."

t_ch: 「君王」牌通常象征着纪律、权威和荣耀，但往往也和孤独、战争画等号。
t_en: The "Ruler" stands for discipline, authority and glory, but frequently also points to isolation and warfare.

t_ch: 纵然汝等愿拥戴我为最后的王，荣耀都归于我……
t_en: Even should you pledge your loyalty to me as the last sovereign, the glory shall be mine and mine alone...

t_ch: 可弑王者，不能再负担起王名：就让垂死的狮子独自走上他的末路吧。
t_en: He who has slain a king can no longer bear the title. Let the withering lion walk his last path alone.

t_ch: 这代表着一位…背离了子民的「君王」吗？
t_en: Is this showing us... a "Ruler" who forsook their people?

t_ch: 哀丽秘榭离流血的世界太远了…这种传奇故事，实在无法不引人遐想啊。
t_en: Stories of such bloodshed like this feel so different from Aedes Elysiae... but they sure can stir the imagination.

t_ch: 翻开「羁客」。
t_en: Reveal the "Traveler."

t_ch: 哦！「羁客」牌，这是最调皮的一张。它常常贴在其他牌的背后出现，擅长伪装成其他卡牌的模样，抽中它可不容易呀！
t_en: Ah! The Traveler, our little trickster! Always hiding behind other cards, disguising itself. What a rare catch!

t_ch: …这里吗？
t_en: ...Over here?

t_ch: 还是——这里？
t_en: Or perhaps — here?

t_ch: 被骗了呀！嘻嘻，眼神和手脚都太慢了，你！
t_en: Gotcha! You'll have to be quicker than that!

t_ch: 好狡黠的声音…像在和人玩捉迷藏？一定得是个擅长捉弄人的家伙才能拿起这张牌吧。
t_en: That teasing voice... just like a child playing hide and seek. Surely, only a master of mischief could pick up this card!

t_ch: 看看别的牌。
t_en: Check the other cards.

t_ch: 翻看第二页上的神谕牌。
t_en: Turn to the second page of oracle cards.

t_ch: 翻开「侍女」。
t_en: Reveal the "Servant."

t_ch: 「侍女」牌，有点轻盈的分量，带着冰凉的安提灵花的香气，就像天上的繁星。
t_en: This card, the Servant, is surprisingly light, bearing the cool scent of Antila flowers, just like starlight.

t_ch: 它象征着新生和希望，却也容易让人联想到挫折、悲伤和空虚。
t_en: It symbolizes new life and hope, yet often brings to mind struggles, sadness, and loss.

t_ch: 那可以被我的双手照拂的花朵…一定生在死亡的荫蔽下吗？
t_en: These precious flowers I care for with my own hands... are they destined to bloom only in death's embrace?

t_ch: 听起来，真是既庄严，又有一丝无法遮盖的哀伤啊……
t_en: It sounds so solemn, yet tinged with an inescapable sorrow...

t_ch: 翻开「医师」。
t_en: Reveal the "Healer."

t_ch: 小巧的「医师」牌，虽然是柔和的颜色，但其中蕴含的勇气不比任何一张牌少，甚至足以融化寒冷的坚冰。
t_en: The humble "Healer" may seem gentle in hue, but its courage matches any other, strong enough to melt even the coldest ice.

t_ch: 我会尽己所能，帮助你们弥合裂隙，努力让阳光洒遍世界上的每一个角落！
t_en: I will do my best to help you mend all fractures and let sunlight shine on every corner of the world!

t_ch: 这回和我勾起手指，就算约定好了，再也不许变卦了哦！
t_en: Make a pinky promise with me. No more going back on your word!

t_ch: 咦，这张牌好像和我的想法很接近：想让自己所能保护的范围，尽可能再大一点……
t_en: Hey, this card really speaks to me. It makes me want to protect as much as I possibly can...

t_ch: 不过，「保护世界上的每一个角落」这种事我可不敢想——那得需要多不可思议的能力啊？
t_en: Still, "protecting everything everywhere" seems like a dream — what amazing power would you need for that?

t_ch: 翻开「学士」。
t_en: Reveal the "Scholar."

t_ch: 这就是有着大魔术师、大表演家、睿智之左眼、{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}的亵渎者等诸多称号的「学士」牌了。
t_en: Behold the Scholar, bearer of many names: the Grand Magician, the Great Performer, the Eye of Wisdom, and the Defiler of {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 啊，原来只有一张么？听着倒像是十几张……
t_en: Oh, there's just one card? With all those titles, I was expecting a whole deck...

t_ch: 还是称呼我为「穿着华服的大地兽」吧——至少它足够精炼，能最强有力地概括我的本质。
t_en: "A dromas draped in finery" will suffice — it's succinct enough to best summarize my true nature.

t_ch: 呃…这个声音，怎么像是能预料到我们在说什么一样？
t_en: Wait, what's with that condescending tone? It's almost as if it knows what we're about to say.

t_ch: 看看别的牌。
t_en: Check the other cards.

t_ch: 翻开白厄的神谕牌。
t_en: Reveal Phainon's oracle cards.

t_ch: 「救世主」…在命运三相神谕的语境下，这张牌意味着谐调和完美无缺。
t_en: The "Deliverer"... According to the tripartite prophecy, this card means absolute harmony and perfection.

t_ch: 而且，这也是所有神谕牌里唯一一张没有负面意象的哦。
t_en: In fact, it's the only oracle card without a dark side.

t_ch: …咦，这次没有神谕牌里的回音了吗？
t_en: What? No divine whispers this time?

t_ch: 想什么呢…这就是你为自己抽出的牌面呀。
t_en: Oh silly... this is simply the card you drew for yourself.

t_ch: 小小的哀丽秘榭，一昔之间降临了无数或尊贵、或传奇的人物：织者、门匠、君王、羁客、侍女、医师、学士……
t_en: The sleepy village of Aedes Elysiae in just one fleeting moment, welcomed an array of legendary figures: the Weaver, Gatekeeper, Ruler, Traveler, Servant, Healer, and Scholar...

t_ch: 他们的话语，和那言语中逐一流露的「心愿」，为哀丽秘榭出身的少年勾勒出对外界最初的想象。
t_en: Their stories and wishes showed the village boy glimpses of a world larger than he'd ever imagined.

t_ch: 那里有纷争的战场，有金色流光的圣城，还有许多他暂时无法理解的话语，和他自幼熟悉的、近乎亘古不变的村子殊异。
t_en: They brought tales of raging battles, of cities crowned in holy light, and of wonders beyond his understanding... each story so far removed from his simple village life.

t_ch: 少年从前只在童年玩伴的神谕牌里，见过这些被书写在纸页上的身份。然而，命运似乎与迷境产生了共鸣……
t_en: Once, these were just images on his friend's oracle cards. And yet, destiny now stirs in resonance with the maze...

t_ch: 现在，他们近在眼前。
t_en: And now, they are right in front of one's eyes.

t_ch: 「救世主」…又是这张牌。
t_en: The Deliverer... this card again.

t_ch: 呵，孩子…看来，你会成为回应世界期许、背负众人心愿的人啊。
t_en: Ah, my child... It seems you're destined to bear the wishes of many, to answer the world's call.

t_ch: 背负…心愿？
t_en: Bear their... wishes?

t_ch: 是啊。正如…那立于山巅的{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: Yes. Just like {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}, standing eternal atop the mountain.

t_ch: …老爹和母亲的心愿很简单，只希望我平安长大，多帮村子里做些好事，少闯祸。
t_en: My parents... they only wished for me to grow up well, to help around the village, stay out of trouble...

t_ch: 皮西厄斯老师希望小校舍能多几卷书，孩子们都能学到有用的知识；小披索希望能早点长成和他老爹一样强壮的猎人……
t_en: Mrs. Pythias wished for more books in her little schoolhouse, wanted us all to learn and grow. And little Piso... he wants to grow up strong like his father, to become a hunter...

t_ch: 我是要…实现他们的愿望吗？
t_en: Is that my purpose? To fulfill their wishes?

t_ch: 也许，对于真正的救世主…这些心愿只是沧海一粟。
t_en: Perhaps... for a true Deliverer, such wishes are but droplets in an ocean.

t_ch: 毕竟刻法勒背负的，是整个世界呢。
t_en: After all, Kephale bears the weight of the entire world.

t_ch: 是啊。真的很难想象，凡人要怎么才能和泰坦相提并论？
t_en: Right... How could any human possibly measure up to a Titan?

t_ch: 嗯，我也很好奇呢。不如……
t_en: Mmm, I wonder too. Perhaps...

t_ch: 这是…？
t_en: What's this...?

t_ch: 它们是神谕牌中揭示的未来。换句话说，是接下来，你生命中会遇见的人。
t_en: What you see are futures written in the cards. Or rather, the people you'll meet on your journey.

t_ch: 我们都很迷茫，对吧？不如和命运聊聊，或许这样，我们才能弄清自己是为了什么踏上旅途……
t_en: Sometimes, fate can guide us when we're lost. Let's consult fate itself. Maybe then we'll know why we must take this path...

t_ch: …还有，一个人究竟要背负多少事物，才能够称得上「救世主」的称号呢。
t_en: And learn... just how many burdens one must bear to become a true "Deliverer."

t_ch: 要说心愿吗？我并无私人的心愿，只有再创世是我唯一的理想…不过，若能编织崭新的未来，我希望凯妮斯和她手下的清洗者永不能进入。
t_en: Personal wishes? I have none. Only the Era Nova matters: The world's rebirth. Yet, if I am to weave a new era, then may it be one where Caenis and her Cleaners shall never tread.

t_ch: 这群丑陋的虫豸，不配假借着「人治」的名头再出现在奥赫玛了。
t_en: These crawling parasites have no place in Okhema, especially not under the deceitful claim of "human rule."

t_ch: 这位…千金大小姐？说起话来让人背后一寒……
t_en: That... noble lady? The way she speaks... it's like ice in my veins...

t_ch: 她是个无私奉献的人。
t_en: She's truly selfless in her dedication.

t_ch: 真的？暂时听不太出来……
t_en: Huh... not exactly the warmth I'd expect from someone selfless...

t_ch: 她是个冷酷淡漠的人。
t_en: She's as cold as winter frost.

t_ch: 嗯，从语气也能猜出几分……
t_en: That's exactly the feeling I'm getting...

t_ch: 吾师，不妨也说说你的心愿吧。
t_en: Teacher, perhaps you could share your wishes with us?

t_ch: 小白，到了这个时候，你还要问*我们*的心愿吗？
t_en: Oh Snowy... you wanna know about our wishes now?

t_ch: 我想在西风尽头的花海，和率先辞别的缇安重逢。
t_en: I wish to see Trianne again — who was the first to depart — in the sea of flowers where the west wind ends.

t_ch: 然后，阿雅，我想看到你真正放下金丝纺成的心茧，像从前雅努萨波利斯的神庙前，那个身穿白裙、眼眸明亮的少女一样，再对*我们*笑一次。
t_en: And Agy, I want to see you shed that golden-thread cocoon around your heart. Just once more, smile at us again like that bright-eyed girl in white who stood before the Janusopolis temple.

t_ch: 最后，希望所有迷途人的跫音不再徘徊不前，取而代之的是祈祷的明亮歌声。
t_en: Finally, I pray that the footsteps of lost souls no longer wander aimlessly, instead, replaced by the bright songs of prayer.

t_ch: *我们*的心愿是不是有点贪心？就当作代替了缇安和缇宁她们，一共说出了三份心愿吧！
t_en: Are we wishing for too much? Well, let's say these three wishes represent Trianne and Trinnon too!

t_ch: 听起来都是很好的心愿，连我也会心中生出向往。
t_en: Those wishes... are truly wonderful. They even stir something deep inside me...

t_ch: 那*我们*就放心啦，因为这是只有「你」才能替*我们*实现的心愿哦。
t_en: Then we can rest easy, because only "you" can make these wishes come true.

t_ch: 只有我…？
t_en: Only... me?

t_ch: 嗯，小白，记好啦！我们……
t_en: Mark these words, Snowy! See you...

t_ch: 明天见！
t_en: ...tomorrow!

t_ch: 白厄？你怎么是这副怯步不前的模样，还问出「心愿是什么」这种幼稚的话。
t_en: Phainon? Since when did you become so timid, asking such juvenile questions about wishes?

t_ch: 啊？我怎么了，有哪里不对吗？反倒是你这副上来就瞧不起人的口气……
t_en: What's that supposed to mean? And what's with that condescending tone...

t_ch: 好伙伴，别说我和这个不穿上衣，气焰还这么嚣张的家伙——未来会很熟啊？
t_en: No way I become friends with this half-naked showoff, right?

t_ch: 让你失望了，你们真的很熟。
t_en: Sorry to disappoint, but you end up being close friends.

t_ch: 我到底会经历什么啊？
t_en: Just what kind of life am I going to lead...

t_ch: 是一起泡过高温浴池那种熟。
t_en: You'll be the kind of friends who think nothing of sitting shoulder to shoulder in a hot bath.

t_ch: 可怕…未来的我还有这种爱好呢？
t_en: Horrifying... is that really something I'll enjoy one day?

t_ch: 你在自言自语什么？罢了……
t_en: What are you mumbling to yourself now? Whatever...

t_ch: 悬锋一族战士的心愿都已经了却，至于我——
t_en: The wishes of the Kremnoan warriors are done. But I—

t_ch: 身经万死，可仍未能抵挡刺穿后背的一剑，来自那漆黑面具的盗火行者。
t_en: I survived a thousand deaths only to fall to a coward's blade... That masked Flame Reaver who struck from behind.

t_ch: 白厄……
t_en: Phainon, you look...

t_ch: 算了，看你一脸迷茫，恐怕什么也没搞明白。
t_en: Never mind. That blank look tells me you're completely lost.

t_ch: 告诉你我现在的心愿吧：要是能有和他决斗的机会，我会要求他堂堂正正地与我正面厮杀，并且，我要亲手斩落他的面具。
t_en: My wish is simple: One honest duel. Face to face, blade to blade. Then I'll strip away that mask with my own hands.

t_ch: 又是杀啊又是死的，这人的愿望还真是野蛮。
t_en: Nothing but violence and vengeance... this person's wish is nothing short of brutal.

t_ch: 但你瞳孔中的怒意，又像极了书中描写的骄傲王族…这份心愿，我记下了。
t_en: Still... those eyes burn with the noble pride I've read about. This wish, I shall not forget.

t_ch: 哟！救世小子来啦？
t_en: Well, well, if it isn't our little Deliverer!

t_ch: 心愿嘛，这还不简单！咱只想吃吃喝喝，遛遛弯晒晒太阳，再过上一千年这样的小日子。不然攒了一大堆钱，却没能在死之前花出去，多可惜呀！
t_en: My wish? Ha! Just let me wine and dine and soak up the sun for another millennium or two. I mean, what's the point of dying rich if you never lived rich?

t_ch: 蜗居公主，你说对吧？
t_en: Right, Princess Homebody?

t_ch: 赛飞儿阁下，我并不是这样想的。
t_en: Cipher, that's... not how I see it.

t_ch: 我的心愿是…找到生命里缺失的部分，感受与人拥抱的温度。那样的感觉，我已经体会过了，是我最为珍藏的回忆。至于平日里攒下的钱……
t_en: My one wish... is to find that missing piece in my life, to feel someone's embrace once more. That warmth... I've felt it before, and it's my dearest memory. And speaking of riches...

t_ch: 赛飞儿阁下，你的神力网罗了那么多珍宝，不是也一分都没花出去么？
t_en: For someone with all those treasures behind your divine power... you've led a pretty cheap life~

t_ch: 哎，怎么说话呢？你这人，看不出来……
t_en: Ouch, where did that come from...

t_ch: 还挺会戳人痛处。
t_en: Right for the jugular, huh?

t_ch: 树庭女孩呢？
t_en: What about our friend from the Grove?

t_ch: 我的心愿，也早在踏上「天空」之前就已经完成了，把我所拥有的疗愈力量化为暖阳，均等地分给了每一个我珍视、爱护的生命。
t_en: My wish came true before I touched the Sky. I transformed my healing power into gentle sunlight, distributing it equally to every life I cherished and loved.

t_ch: 你俩还真是够*无私*的…难怪阿格莱雅一早就把你们拉入了逐火。
t_en: You two are so selfless, aren't you? No surprise Aglaea wanted you for the Flame-Chase Journey.

t_ch: 说到这里，我想起来了！我啊，曾经还真有一个念头，想尝尝生来就是家财万贯的贵族小姐的滋味。
t_en: You know what? I did have one wild dream, to know what it feels like being born a noble lady, drowning in riches.

t_ch: 要是能让我和那裁缝女的出身换一换，换我做高高在上的大小姐，她做穷人，做小偷，看她还能不能成为现在的样子！我嘛，说不定也会成为「赛金织」！
t_en: Just think, if I traded places with that seamstress! Me in the mansion, her on the streets! I'd like to see if she'd still become the "Goldweaver" then! Maybe I'd even take her place!

t_ch: 扑哧——
她们在开茶话会呢？
t_en: Can we jump in and fit right into their convo?

t_ch: 感觉有点难以融入话题……
t_en: Doesn't sound like an easy task...

t_ch: 都是让人想露出笑容的心愿。
t_en: These wishes... they make you want to smile.

t_ch: 我也是这样想的，这些心愿…并不是沉重的东西，相反，像哀丽秘榭的孩子们喜欢的糖果纸，在有阳光的地方更加闪闪发亮。
t_en: I can't disagree with you. These wishes aren't weights to bear. They're like those candy wrappers the Aedes Elysiae children love, shining even brighter when bathed in sunlight.

t_ch: 「拥抱」、「疗愈」和…嗯，该怎么描述，「无拘无束的生活」？
t_en: "Warm embrace," "equal healing," and... perhaps... "living free"?

t_ch: 总之，应该可以理解为，她们想要一个更温柔的世界吧。
t_en: They're all wishing for a kinder world, in their own way.

t_ch: 呵，白厄，我的好学生，还需要再赘述一遍我留给你的话吗？
t_en: Ah, Phainon, my student, must we go over this again?

t_ch: 记好了，我不喜欢「心愿」这样的字眼，那代表在诸神面前的软弱和趴伏乞怜。
t_en: Listen well. I detest the word "wish." It reeks of mortals crawling before their gods.

t_ch: 我应做的工程已毕，只差你用最后一步践行那最终的实证——
t_en: My life's work is done. All that remains is for you to take the final step and prove the ultimate truth—

t_ch: 我，阿那克萨戈拉斯，将成为新世界的神明。我将以确凿无疑的学说，向人们证明：凡人的思想，也足以成就神的伟业。
t_en: I, Anaxagoras, shall become a god of the new world. Through irrefutable logic, I will prove that mortal wisdom can achieve divine greatness.

t_ch: 他是不是…有点…？
t_en: Isn't he... you know, a little...?

t_ch: 我只是偶尔幻想一下英雄故事罢了，可他居然幻想自己有朝一日会成为…泰坦？
t_en: I mean, I just daydream about hero stories, but he's fantasizing about becoming a... Titan?

t_ch: 小点声，他以后是你的老师。
t_en: Careful — that's your future teacher you're talking about.

t_ch: 这，真是让人心情复杂…我能学会他身上那股自信的劲吗？
t_en: Well, that's... something. Could I ever be that sure of myself?

t_ch: 他的理想全靠你实现了。
t_en: His ambitions rest on your shoulders.

t_ch: 好伙伴，你也是认真的吗？我真的要被托付这种接近于天方夜谭的理想吗？
t_en: Come on, you're joking right? Am I really being burdened with a dream that feels more like a fantasy?

t_ch: 如何？我能感觉到，你内心的迷雾似乎已经散去些许了呢。
t_en: So tell me... has the uncertainty in your heart begun to fade?

t_ch: 真没想到将来会遇到这么多人，还有他们的心愿……
t_en: So many faces in my future... so many wishes...

t_ch: …但，命运怎么会如此揭示呢？
t_en: Still... why would fate choose to show us this path?

t_ch: 在顾虑什么？
t_en: What's weighing on your mind?
